name: Guilded
description: Guilded was a VoIP, instant messaging and digital distribution platform developed by Guilded Inc. and owned by Roblox Corporation.

name: Hang Out in a Disco and Chat
description: Hang Out in a Disco and Chat is a discontinued iOS app developed by Roblox Corporation.

name: Roblox Developer
description: the Roblox Developer App was a mobile app released on the iOS App Store. The app allowed the user to pair their device to Roblox Studio so that they could use it as a testing device when using Server-Client mode.

name: Survive the Disasters
description: Survive the Disasters was an action video game published by Roblox Corporation. It simulated Stickmasterluke's Natural Disaster Survival experience.

name: Bloxxer
description: the Bloxxer badge was awarded to players who reached at least 250 knockouts and had more knockouts than wipeouts.

name: Combat Initiation
description: the Combat Initiation badge was a badge obtainable by getting 10 Knockouts on a game which used the classic Roblox Leaderboard script.

name: Warrior
description: the Warrior badge was a Roblox badge given to players who had at least 100 knockouts.

name: Welcome To The Club
description: the Welcome to The Club badge was a Roblox badge that could have been obtained if an individual had purchased any type of Builders Club.

name: 13+ Avatar Items
description: specific items on the Avatar Marketplace could only be purchased by players who were 13 years of age or older.

name: All Things Roblox
description: All Things Roblox was one of the four Roblox section subforums.

name: BC Exclusive items
description: BC Exclusive items could only be bought by people with Builders Club membership.

name: Best Friends
description: Best Friends was a feature where users could set 10 users to be their best friends. When a user was set as a best friend, users could see their status updates via the feed, which was originally on the profile.

name: Bevels
description: Bevels are a partially discontinued graphical Roblox feature, which rendered smooth, 45-degree edges on meshless Parts.

name: Comments
description: Comments, formerly known as Commentary, was a section available through assets on the Roblox website.

name: Engagement-Based Payouts
description: Engagement-Based Payouts (formerly Premium Payout) was a feature which allow developers to be paid in Robux based on how much time Premium subscribers spend in your game.

name: Faces
description: Faces were Marketplace assets that changed the image of an avatar's head.

name: Gift accessories
description: gift accessories were an avatar item that would "unwrap" or turn into a different avatar item, like a gift. Users would purchase the gift not knowing what would come out of it until it was opened.

name: Group walls
description: group walls were a feature that allowed group members to post messages and interact within their group's dedicated space.

name: Guests
description: Guests were a feature which allowed unregistered players to test the Roblox platform without a formal account.

name: MEGA places
description: MEGA places were games whose servers could hold up to 50 players or more with a reduced amount of lag than normal due to increased capabilities of the computation power with additional players.

name: My Feed
description: My Feed was a feature on Roblox where players could receive updates to their status such as latest shouts from groups they were in, receive updates from games if they were following them, as well as receive status updates from users on their friends list.

name: Outlines
description: outlines were dark, pixel-shaded lines that appeared around the edges of Parts.

name: Personal Servers
description: Personal Servers (also known as Personal Build Servers) allowed users to create their own Places with other users by giving them permission.

name: Player Points
description: Player Points were a scoring system on Roblox, generally intended to display players' skill.

name: Sets
description: sets were groups of models or decals that were created by players on Roblox, allowing players to easily find needed models in one, easy-to-find location.

name: Star Codes
description: Star Codes were a feature that was implemented on April 29, 2019. Its main purpose is to support and feature those in the Roblox Video Stars Program.

name: Super Safe Chat
description: Super Safe Chat was a feature added on March 27, 2007, to allow Roblox to comply with COPPA. It prevented under-thirteen users from typing custom messages in the chat, to help prevent these users from seeing/typing expletives and/or profanity.

name: System badges
description: system badges were displayed on profiles and showcased achievements earned on the Roblox site, including visit milestones, friendships, and combat scripts which were retired in 2015.

name: Tix
description: a Ticket, also known as Tix for short, was a former Roblox currency alongside Robux, introduced on August 2, 2007.

name: User advertisements
description: user advertisements  were a community-driven feature that allowed players to create and run their own banners, skyscrapers, and squares to promote games, clothing, or groups.

name: Voxel terrain
description: voxel terrain, also known as legacy terrain or simply old terrain, was the original terrain system. The terrain was made up of many individual cells with simple shapes such as ramps, cubes and corner ramps.

name: Ambassador program
description: The Ambassador Program acted as a Roblox referral system members to gain more Tickets.

name: Builders Club
description: Builders Club (BC) was a Roblox membership split into 3 tiers that granted users extra privileges.

name: Clans
description: Clans were groups of people that could compete for the top spot on a game's Leaderboard. They got to the top spot by earning Player points from that game.

name: Forums
description: the Roblox Forums were used for discussing and socializing with other people in a text-based format. The forums housed different subforums that each had their purposes.

name: Luobu
description: Luobu was the mainland Chinese version of Roblox.

name: RoblEX
description: the ROBLOX Trade Currency (or RoblEX) was a section of the site used to trade both currencies, Robux and Tickets with different rates.

name: Roblox Premium
description: Roblox Premium is an outgoing paid membership that gives users perks and benefits.

name: Xbox One S Roblox Bundle
description: The Xbox One S Roblox Bundle was a Xbox One S bundle created by Microsoft for the Xbox One S. Purchasing the bundle granted exclusive Rthro avatar bundles, avatar accessories, and Robux.

name: m.roblox.com
description: m.roblox.com used to be a variant of the mobile version of the Roblox site.

name: Roblox Developer Hub
description: the Roblox Developer Hub was an official resource about Roblox development. It gathers tutorials and guides on game development and has an API reference with documentation on classes, enums, and the global namespace.

name: Roblox Developer Wiki
description: the Roblox Developer Wiki was the official resource about Roblox development. It gathered tutorials and guides on game development and had an API reference with documentation on classes and the global namespace.

name: The Bird Says____.
description: The Bird Says____. is a shoulder accessory that could be obtained for free by redeeming the promotional code "TWEETROBLOX".

name: Toys
description: Roblox toys were physical real life small collectible mini-figures and playsets based on various notable characters, users, experiences and other elements of the Roblox universe.

name: Classic Faces
description: Classic Faces were Marketplace assets that changed the image of an avatar's head.

name: Accelerator Program
description: the Accelerator Program was a professional development program where creators worked at Roblox HQ to build new experiences.

name: Live-Ops
description: Live-Ops was a feature that highlighted in-game events and updates on the Roblox website.

name: Roblox Badges
description: Roblox badges, also known as System Badges, were a feature separate from user badges that could be earned across the website for various achievements.

name: Knockouts
description: Knockouts were a statistic on the classic Roblox leaderboard that tracked how many times a player had defeated another player.

name: Wipeouts
description: Wipeouts were a statistic on the classic Roblox leaderboard that tracked how many times a player had been defeated.

name: Impulse Tracker Modules
description: Impulse Tracker modules (.it files) were an audio format that Roblox used to support for background music in experiences.

name: Upload to YouTube
description: Upload to YouTube was a feature that allowed users to automatically upload their in-game video recordings to YouTube with pre-set titles and descriptions.

name: ImPlicitNgine
description: ImPlicitNgine was the original rendering engine used by Roblox before it was replaced by more modern systems.

name: Image Moderator Badge
description: the Image Moderator badge was a system badge given to users who were part of the image moderation team.

name: Top Poster Feature
description: the Top Poster feature was a forum system that highlighted users who were the most active contributors to the Roblox forums.

name: Super Moderator
description: Super Moderator was a high-level moderation rank on Roblox, often symbolized by a system badge.

name: Facebook Connect
description: Facebook Connect was a feature that allowed users to link their Roblox accounts with Facebook to find friends and share achievements.

name: Forum Moderator
description: Forum Moderator was a role given to users who helped moderate the official Roblox forums.

name: Roblox Talk
description: Roblox Talk was a subforum dedicated to general discussions about the Roblox platform.

name: Roblox Points
description: Roblox Points, also known as Player Points, were a scoring system intended to display players' skill and contribution to experiences.

name: Roblox Outrageous Cast
description: Roblox Outrageous Cast was an official video series or podcast produced by Roblox staff.

name: ROBLOX Browser
description: the ROBLOX Browser was a dedicated web browser built into the Roblox client for navigating the website.

name: Experimental Mode
description: Experimental Mode was a setting for experiences that did not have FilteringEnabled, which was eventually removed to improve platform security.

name: Clans & Guilds
description: Clans were a sub-feature of groups that allowed for competitive play and leaderboard tracking.

name: Physics-Based Collision Sounds
description: Physics-Based Collision Sounds were automatic sound effects triggered when parts collided in the physics engine.

name: Play Solo
description: Play Solo was a mode in Roblox Studio that allowed developers to test their experiences locally without a server.

name: Bonus Round
description: Bonus Round was a short-lived feature or event on the Roblox website.
